opp.name: Provincial Treasury S2P - Opp
opp.next_step: 17 April, 2026: RfP has been submitted in collaboration with EY
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope contains some AI in product scope (Merlin, ML iAnalyze) at $475K Formal Evaluation stage ‚Äî but no ANA, no Agentic AI, no Merlin Intake. The deal is mostly a traditional S2P stack (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSource, iSupplier) with AI bundled as add-ons rather than headlined. The breadth of traditional modules suggests this is a coverage-driven Provincial Treasury procurement, not an AI-led modernisation.
• Zero Avoma meetings on file and BD_Engagement is Unengaged despite the Formal Evaluation stage. Federal/Government deals are typically RFP-driven with limited rep-buyer dialogue captured in tools like Avoma. The Description is null in SFDC. There is no captured rep-buyer transcript from which to extract any AI sentiment.
• Low confidence: no Description, no call data. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4 boundary given the Federal/Government context (typically procedural RFP) and no AI champion evidence. Would move UP only if an issued RFP explicitly mandates AI/agentic capability ‚Äî common in Federal modernisation programmes, so monitor the upcoming RFP content.

items.quote: Zero buyer calls discovered across opp+account+attendee Avoma pulls; no transcripts, no notes
Zero OpportunityContactRole records; no buyer-side contacts mapped to this opportunity
SOQL Q3 returned no custom MEDDPICC fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, etc. not present in this org or all empty)
Competitors__c empty, zero calls, zero tasks/events with competitive mentions, Next_Step_History__c not present
RFP submitted 17 April 2026 but Zycus has no record of the RFP criteria, weighting, or evaluation timeline

sources: SOQL Q3: Customer_Business_Problem__c and related fields not present or empty
metrics.narrative: No business problem, quantified savings, ROI case, or pain articulation on record. The RFP scope (S2P: iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) implies procurement-cycle efficiency and supplier-management pain, but no buyer has stated the current cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or savings target. With zero buyer calls and zero free-text fields populated (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all queried and returned empty or not present in this org), there is no confirmed pain owner, no quantified value case, and no compelling event. The move is to secure a discovery or RFP debrief call with the procurement lead or CFO-office sponsor to surface and quantify the pain, then build the ROI model with EY.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Zero OpportunityContactRole records, so no one is tagged as Champion or coach. Zero buyer calls means no advocacy evidence (no introductions, no internal-doc sharing, no competitive deflection, no agenda-driving on our behalf). The account contact roster lists 20 buyer-side contacts across gauteng.gov.za, ectreasury.gov.za, siyakhana.co.za, and gautrain.co.za domains, including director-level procurement and finance roles, but none are confirmed participants in THIS opportunity's RFP cycle (no calls, no tasks, no emails, no contact roles). EY is the collaboration partner per Next_Step__c, but a partner is not a champion (a champion is an internal buyer advocate). The 85-day silence and zero stakeholder engagement signals no champion is actively working the deal internally. The move is to ask EY who the internal advocate or procurement project lead is at the treasury, then build a direct relationship with that person via a debrief or clarification call, and assess their willingness to coach us on the evaluation progress and competitive landscape.

sources: SOQL Q3: competition fields not present or empty
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. Competitors__c field is empty (per ground truth 'unknown'). Zero Avoma calls means no buyer has named a shortlist, no competitive comparisons have been discussed, and no RFP shortlist has been shared. The free-text competition fields (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c) were queried in Q3 and are not present in this org or returned empty. Zero tasks/events and Next_Step_History__c (queried, not present or empty) means no competitive intel logged. In a provincial-treasury S2P RFP in South Africa the typical competitors are SAP Ariba (incumbent in many SA government orgs), Coupa, Oracle, and sometimes local/BEE-compliant platforms or manual/Excel (do-nothing). The RFP process implies at least 3-5 vendors were invited. The move is to ask EY who else submitted (EY will know the shortlist if they collaborated on Zycus's response), then request competitive positioning guidance from the Zycus SA team and product marketing (how to win against Ariba/Coupa on government compliance, BEE, cost, and Merlin differentiation).

sources: SOQL Q3: pain-related fields not present or empty
identify_pain.narrative: No confirmed pain. The product scope (S2P suite including Merlin) implies operational pain: manual procurement processes, poor contract visibility, slow sourcing cycles, supplier-management fragmentation, invoice/AP bottlenecks, and lack of negotiation intelligence. In a provincial treasury these typically translate to budget leakage, compliance risk (irregular expenditure under PFMA), delayed service delivery, and audit findings. However, zero buyer calls and zero free-text field content (Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c all queried, not present or empty) means no buyer has articulated the pain, no pain owner is named, and no urgency/compelling event is documented. The RFP issuance itself is a weak signal of acknowledged pain, but the 10-week silence suggests either the pain is not urgent or Zycus is out of the conversation. The move is to ask EY what pain drivers surfaced in the RFP scoping, then get a direct call with the treasury procurement or finance lead to confirm the pain, quantify it, and anchor it to a fiscal-year or audit deadline.

sources: SOQL Q3: paper-process fields not present or empty
Next_Step__c: EY collaboration (SI role inferred, not confirmed)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process is unknown. Government contracts in South Africa follow PFMA (Public Finance Management Act) and treasury regulations: RFP, evaluation, award, then contracting (typically a multi-year SaaS MSA or a fixed-term supply agreement, plus implementation SOW). InfoSec review, POPIA (data protection) compliance, and ERP integration (likely SAP or similar) are mandatory gates. Zero buyer calls, zero field content (Current_Contract_Expiration__c queried and empty, no Mandate__c or legal/procurement-channel fields), and zero tasks/events related to contracting mean the MSA template, the legal review lead, the InfoSec contact, the POPIA data-residency requirement, and the ERP integration scope are all unknown. EY may be the implementation partner (SI role implied by 'collaboration'), but that is unconfirmed. The move is to ask EY what the contracting and implementation plan is, and to request the treasury's standard MSA template and InfoSec questionnaire so legal can prep in parallel with the RFP evaluation.

sources: SOQL Q3: EB-related fields not present or empty
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Zero OpportunityContactRole records; no one tagged as EB, Executive Sponsor, or budget authority. The free-text fields queried for budget signaling (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) are not present in this org or returned empty. Zero buyer calls means no commercial or pricing conversation has occurred to reveal who controls the budget. In a provincial treasury (government) deal, the EB is typically the CFO, Head of Treasury, or Deputy Director General (finance). The account contact roster includes Owen Witbooi (Deputy Director General), but his involvement in THIS opportunity's scope is unconfirmed (no call, no contact role, no task). The move is to ask EY (the RFP collaboration partner per Next_Step) who the budget authority and approval chain are, then book a commercial scoping call with that person.

sources: Next_Step__c: RfP submitted 17 April 2026 with EY
SOQL Q3: decision-process fields not present or empty
decision_process.narrative: A government RFP process is typically: RFP issue, vendor submission, evaluation committee scoring, shortlist, finalist presentations/demos, reference checks, approval by treasury board or executive, contract award. The RFP was submitted 17 April 2026 per Next_Step__c, so the deal is now in the evaluation-committee-scoring window (expected 4-8 weeks in SA public sector), but 10 weeks have passed with no update, which is either normal slow government pace or a stall. The approval chain in a provincial treasury usually runs: procurement specialist → director (supply chain) → CFO / head of treasury → MEC (Member of Executive Council, the political head) for large procurements. Zero buyer calls and zero field content (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c queried, not present or empty) means the committee composition, the scoring timeline, the shortlist date, and the approval thresholds are unknown. EY may know the process milestones. The move is to ask EY for the evaluation timeline and committee feedback window, and to request a post-submission debrief or clarification-question session with the treasury procurement lead.

sources: Next_Step__c: RfP submitted 17 April 2026 with EY
SOQL Q3: decision-criteria fields not present or empty
decision_criteria.narrative: The RFP itself (submitted 17 April 2026 per Next_Step__c) IS the decision criteria, but Zycus has not seen the RFP document, the evaluation scorecard, or the weighting. The product scope (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) signals the functional criteria: contract lifecycle management, sourcing automation, spend analytics (ML), supplier collaboration, P2P, AP automation, and AI/Merlin negotiation intelligence. However, zero buyer calls and zero field content (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c queried, not present or empty) means the relative weighting, the mandatory vs nice-to-have split, the AI/Merlin appetite level, the implementation timeline expectation, and the price/TCO ceiling are unknown. EY collaborated on the RFP submission, so EY may hold the criteria detail. The move is to request the RFP scoring matrix and evaluation timeline from EY or directly from the treasury procurement lead, then map our response against it.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. AIS fields are null (per ground truth), and zero buyer calls mean no direct evidence of AI appetite. Merlin is in scope (Products__c), which implies the RFP response positioned AI/negotiation intelligence, but whether the buyer values it, understands it, or sees it as a risk is undocumented. The move is to clarify with EY what the treasury's AI maturity and interest level is, and whether Merlin was a positive differentiator or a concern in the RFP evaluation.

items.change: RFP submitted in collaboration with EY (per Next_Step__c)
85 days of silence: no buyer calls, no field updates, no tasks/events, no stakeholder engagement

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 01 April 2026, RFP submitted 17 April 2026 with EY per Next_Step__c (~70 days ago), then went silent. LastActivityDate 02 April 2026 (85 days ago). No field-history changes to Stage, Amount, CloseDate, or ForecastCategory in trailing 90 days. No tasks, no events, no buyer calls, no stakeholder updates. The server engagement pulse is COOLING (85 days since last activity). This is a post-RFP stall: the evaluation window in SA government is typically 4-8 weeks, so at 10 weeks either the process is delayed, Zycus missed the shortlist, or the deal team lost the thread. The RFP submission itself is the only confirmed forward step; everything after is dark.

items.detail: RFP submitted 17 April 2026, then 85 days of silence. No shortlist notification, no evaluation feedback, no next-step update. Either the government RFP process is delayed (normal in SA public sector but a risk to the April 2027 close date if it drags into Q4 2026), or Zycus missed the shortlist and the deal is lost but not yet marked closed-lost.

Zero buyer engagement for 85 days and zero stakeholder contact roles mean Zycus has no visibility into the RFP evaluation committee, the scoring progress, the shortlist, or the competitive standing. If the deal is still live, Zycus is completely out of the conversation and cannot influence the outcome. If a competitor has a champion on the evaluation committee, Zycus will lose by default.

EY is the collaboration partner per Next_Step__c (RfP submitted in collaboration with EY), but EY's role, deliverables, and engagement status are undefined. If EY was only a proposal-writing consultant and is now disengaged, Zycus has no local delivery partner or government-deal coach. If EY is the intended implementation SI but has not been formally contracted or scoped, that is a delivery-risk gap. The move is to confirm EY's ongoing involvement and role (SI, proposal support only, or champion-builder).

Government RFP in South Africa: BEE (Black Economic Empowerment) compliance, POPIA data residency, SAP ERP integration, and local support are typically weighted heavily. Zycus's positioning on these in the RFP response is undocumented. If any were weak, a competitor with stronger BEE credentials, local data centers, or incumbent SAP integration will score higher. The move is to validate with EY and Zycus SA team that the RFP response covered these, and prepare clarifications or enhancements if needed.

champion_strength.source: Zero OpportunityContactRole records, zero Avoma calls, 85-day silence
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Zero OpportunityContactRole records, zero buyer calls, and 85 days of silence mean no internal advocate is mapped or engaged. EY is the collaboration partner per Next_Step__c but is not a champion (a champion is an internal buyer advocate at the treasury). The account contact roster includes 20 buyer-side contacts, but none are confirmed participants in this RFP cycle. Without a champion to coach on evaluation progress, competitive standing, and approval-chain navigation, Zycus is flying blind in a government RFP. The move is to identify and build a champion: ask EY who the procurement project lead or evaluation committee chair is, then engage that person directly to debrief the RFP, offer clarifications, and assess their willingness to advocate internally.

items.action: Re-engage EY to confirm Zycus is still in the RFP evaluation and get the shortlist and timeline.
items.trigger: RFP submitted 17 April 2026 with EY collaboration, then 85 days of silence with no shortlist notification or evaluation feedback.
items.expected_effect: Confirms whether Zycus is still in consideration or has been eliminated; if still live, surfaces the shortlist competitors, the evaluation timeline, and any gaps in the submission that need clarification or strengthening. If eliminated, closes the loop and allows the deal to be marked closed-lost. Critical first step to restart momentum after 10 weeks of silence.

items.action: Request the RFP scoring matrix and evaluation criteria from the treasury procurement lead.
items.trigger: RFP submitted but criteria, weighting, and scoring process unknown; zero buyer calls to discuss evaluation.
items.expected_effect: Reveals the decision criteria (price vs functionality vs AI vs BEE vs local support weighting), Zycus's scoring vs shortlist competitors, and any gaps or concerns flagged by the evaluation committee. Allows targeted positioning and clarification to address weaknesses before shortlist decisions finalize. Also signals Zycus is engaged and responsive, which builds trust.

items.action: Map and engage the Economic Buyer and evaluation committee chair at the treasury.
items.trigger: Zero buyer-side contact roles, no EB identified, 85 days of silence; unknown who controls budget and RFP approval.
items.expected_effect: Identifies the budget authority (likely CFO, Head of Treasury, or Deputy Director General for a R4.75M deal) and the procurement project lead who chairs the evaluation committee. Books a debrief or clarification call to restart the relationship, surface any concerns or gaps in the RFP response, and build rapport. Converts the procurement lead into a coach who will provide competitive intel and advocate for Zycus through the approval chain. Critical to unblock the 85-day stall and regain control of the deal.

items.action: Add the key treasury stakeholders as OpportunityContactRole records and multi-thread beyond a single contact.
items.trigger: Zero buyer-side contact roles on this opp (DATA-HYGIENE GAP per ground truth); account roster lists 20+ contacts but none mapped to THIS opportunity.
items.expected_effect: Creates the stakeholder baseline so the deal team knows who to engage. Identifies which of the 20 account contacts are involved in THIS RFP scope (procurement directors, finance leads, evaluation committee members) and logs them as contact roles. Enables multi-threading (engaging multiple stakeholders in parallel) to reduce single-point-of-failure risk and surface competitive intel from multiple perspectives. This is an action item to unblock stakeholder engagement, not CRM housekeeping.

items.action: Validate BEE compliance, POPIA data residency, and SAP integration positioning with EY and Zycus SA team.
items.trigger: SA government RFPs typically weight BEE, POPIA, ERP integration, and local support heavily; Zycus positioning on these is undocumented.
items.expected_effect: Confirms the RFP response adequately addressed BEE credentials, POPIA data-residency commitments, SAP integration depth, and local implementation/support capability. If any were weak, prepares a post-RFP clarification or value-add (e.g. a BEE partner proposal, a POPIA compliance brief, a SAP integration reference from another SA government client) to strengthen the position before shortlist decisions finalize. Reduces execution risk and increases win probability against competitors with stronger local credentials.

confidence_signals.summary: Zero confidence signals. No buyer calls, no stakeholder engagement, no champion, no EB/DM mapped, 85 days of silence post-RFP, and no response to the submission. The only positive is the RFP was submitted (a formal buying step), but the 10-week silence with no shortlist notification is a strong negative signal (either Zycus missed the cut, or the process stalled and no one is working it). The forecast_category Pipeline is appropriate given the uncertainty, but even that is optimistic without confirmation Zycus is still in the running.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 01 April 2026 (86 days ago), stage Formal Evaluation since creation. Close date 01 April 2027 is 279 days out. LastActivityDate 02 April 2026 (85 days ago). Time in Formal Evaluation ~86 days with no forward movement visible. RFP submission logged 17 April 2026 (~70 days ago) but no subsequent buyer touch, response, shortlist signal, or next-step update. Zero matched buyer calls discovered across opp+account+attendee pulls. A 10-week silence post-RFP in a government procurement is a stall, not normal cycle time. No forward slip yet because the close date is distant, but momentum has stopped.

north_star_verdict.headline: RFP submitted 17 April via EY, followed by 85 days of total buyer silence—no shortlist notification, no stakeholder contact, no process update. Deal is stalled in procurement limbo; must confirm through EY whether Zycus remains in contention or missed the.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a post-RFP dark deal: 85 days since last activity, zero buyer engagement, zero stakeholders mapped, unknown competitive shortlist, and no champion. The three highest-leverage win-strategy moves are: (1) re-engage immediately via EY to confirm Zycus is still in the RFP evaluation and get the shortlist/timeline; (2) map the treasury decision-makers (EB, procurement lead, evaluation committee chair) and book a debrief or clarification call to restart the relationship; (3) build a government-deal champion internally at the treasury (a procurement director or finance lead who will coach on the evaluation progress and advocate for Zycus through the approval chain).

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. Zero intel from calls, fields, or tasks. In a provincial-treasury S2P RFP in South Africa the likely field is SAP Ariba (often the incumbent in SA government), Coupa, Oracle, and possibly local/BEE platforms or a do-nothing/Excel baseline. Without the RFP shortlist or buyer feedback, Zycus's standing is unconfirmed. EY collaboration on the submission is a positive (credibility, local delivery capability), but 10 weeks of post-RFP silence with no shortlist notification is a negative signal (either Zycus missed the cut, or the process stalled). The single highest-priority move is to confirm via EY or the treasury whether Zycus is still in consideration, and if so, who the shortlist competitors are.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin (AI negotiation agent) is in the product scope per Products__c, which is a strong signal of AI positioning in the RFP response. However, with zero buyer calls, zero AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null per ground truth), and zero field content on AI appetite or needs (AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c queried, not present), the buyer's AI interest level and Merlin's role in the evaluation are unknown. Government buyers in SA are typically AI-curious but cautious (regulatory, change-management, and skills concerns). If Merlin was a differentiator in the RFP response, Zycus should confirm the evaluation committee understood and valued it; if it was undersold or seen as risky, that could be a gap. The move is to ask EY how the AI/Merlin component was received in the RFP scoping, and to prepare a Merlin proof-of-value (e.g. a negotiation-savings case study from another government or public-sector client) to reinforce differentiation in any shortlist presentation.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is unconfirmed. The RFP scope (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) matches Zycus's core S2P suite, so functional coverage is strong on paper. However, zero buyer calls and zero field content mean the evaluation criteria, the weighting (e.g. price vs functionality vs AI vs local support vs BEE compliance), and any gaps identified during pre-RFP discussions are unknown. Government RFPs in SA often prioritize BEE credentials, data residency (POPIA), ERP integration (SAP), and local support; Zycus's standing on these dimensions is not documented. The 10-week silence suggests either a fit issue surfaced in evaluation, or the process is simply slow. The move is to get the RFP scoring feedback from the treasury or EY to identify any exposure areas, then address them proactively.

• Product scope contains some AI in product scope (Merlin, ML iAnalyze) at $475K Formal Evaluation stage ‚Äî but no ANA, no Agentic AI, no Merlin Intake. The deal is mostly a traditional S2P stack (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSource, iSupplier) with AI bundled as add-ons rather than headlined. The breadth of traditional modules suggests this is a coverage-driven Provincial Treasury procurement, not an AI-led modernisation.
• Zero Avoma meetings on file and BD_Engagement is Unengaged despite the Formal Evaluation stage. Federal/Government deals are typically RFP-driven with limited rep-buyer dialogue captured in tools like Avoma. The Description is null in SFDC. There is no captured rep-buyer transcript from which to extract any AI sentiment.
• Low confidence: no Description, no call data. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4 boundary given the Federal/Government context (typically procedural RFP) and no AI champion evidence. Would move UP only if an issued RFP explicitly mandates AI/agentic capability ‚Äî common in Federal modernisation programmes, so monitor the upcoming RFP content.

value.detail: RFP submitted 17 April 2026, then 85 days of silence. No shortlist notification, no evaluation feedback, no next-step update. Either the government RFP process is delayed (normal in SA public sector but a risk to the April 2027 close date if it drags into Q4 2026), or Zycus missed the shortlist and the deal is lost but not yet marked closed-lost.
value.source: Next_Step__c, LastActivityDate 02 April 2026, server engagement pulse COOLING

packets.subject: RFP submitted 17 April 2026, then 85 days of silence. No shortlist notification, no evaluation feedback, no next-step update. Either the government RFP process is delayed (normal in SA public sector but a risk to the April 2027 close date if it drags into Q4 2026), or Zycus missed the shortlist and the deal is lost but not yet marked closed-lost.

value.detail: Zero buyer engagement for 85 days and zero stakeholder contact roles mean Zycus has no visibility into the RFP evaluation committee, the scoring progress, the shortlist, or the competitive standing. If the deal is still live, Zycus is completely out of the conversation and cannot influence the outcome. If a competitor has a champion on the evaluation committee, Zycus will lose by default.
value.source: Zero OpportunityContactRole, zero Avoma calls, 85-day silence

value.detail: EY is the collaboration partner per Next_Step__c (RfP submitted in collaboration with EY), but EY's role, deliverables, and engagement status are undefined. If EY was only a proposal-writing consultant and is now disengaged, Zycus has no local delivery partner or government-deal coach. If EY is the intended implementation SI but has not been formally contracted or scoped, that is a delivery-risk gap. The move is to confirm EY's ongoing involvement and role (SI, proposal support only, or champion-builder).

value.detail: Government RFP in South Africa: BEE (Black Economic Empowerment) compliance, POPIA data residency, SAP ERP integration, and local support are typically weighted heavily. Zycus's positioning on these in the RFP response is undocumented. If any were weak, a competitor with stronger BEE credentials, local data centers, or incumbent SAP integration will score higher. The move is to validate with EY and Zycus SA team that the RFP response covered these, and prepare clarifications or enhancements if needed.
value.source: Inference from deal geography (SA government), zero field content

value.flag: ZERO buyer-side OpportunityContactRole records on this opp (DATA-HYGIENE GAP per ground truth). Add the key treasury procurement and finance stakeholders (director-level and above) as contact roles, and multi-thread beyond a single point of contact. The account roster lists 20+ contacts across gauteng.gov.za, ectreasury.gov.za, siyakhana.co.za, and gautrain.co.za; identify which are involved in THIS RFP scope and log them. This is an action item to unblock stakeholder engagement, not CRM housekeeping.

value.flag: No champion or internal coach at the treasury. In a government RFP a champion (internal advocate) is critical to navigate the evaluation committee, surface competitive intel, and push Zycus through the approval gates. The 85-day silence signals no one internally is working the deal for us. The move is to ask EY who the procurement project lead or evaluation committee chair is, book a call with that person to debrief the RFP submission, provide any clarifications, and assess their willingness to coach us on next steps and competitive standing. Build trust and responsiveness to convert them into a coach.

value.flag: Government-deal execution risk: BEE compliance, data residency (POPIA), ERP integration (likely SAP), and local support are typically weighted heavily in SA public-sector RFPs, but Zycus's positioning on these is undocumented. The move is to confirm with EY and the Zycus SA team that the RFP response addressed BEE credentials, POPIA data-residency commitments, SAP integration depth, and local implementation/support capability. If any of these were weak in the submission, prepare a post-RFP clarification or value-add (e.g. a BEE partner proposal, a POPIA compliance brief, a SAP integration reference) to strengthen the position before shortlist decisions finalize.

Zero OpportunityContactRole records: no buyer stakeholders, no champion, no EB, no DM mapped to this opportunity.

OpportunityFieldHistory trailing 365d shows zero changes to Stage, Amount, CloseDate, ForecastCategory: deal has been static since creation.
RFP document, scoring matrix, evaluation timeline, and shortlist unknown: submitted 17 April 2026 per Next_Step__c but no follow-up or feedback logged.
